basic_details.job_title: MPPSC Assistant Director Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 71 Posts
basic_details.post_name: Assistant Director, Farmer Welfare & Agriculture Development Department (Field & Extension)
basic_details.short_information: Madhya Pradesh Public Service Commission (MPPSC) has released an official notification for the recruitment of 71 Assistant Director Posts. Interested and eligible candidates can apply Online through the official MPPSC website. The last date to submit the application form is 24-05-2026. In this article, you’ll find the MPPSC Assistant Director Posts recruitment details, including eligibility criteria, age limit, salary structure, selection process, application steps, and direct links to the official notification and Online application form.

application_fee.details: Payment Mode: Online through Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or UPI Portal Fee: ₹50/- (Non-Refundable) for all categories Correction Fee Period: 25.05.2026 to 31.05.2026 (till 12:00 noon) - ₹3,000/- Final Submission Fee Period: 01.06.2026 to 28.10.2026 (till 12:00 noon) - ₹25,000/- Application fees once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances Candidates must keep the payment receipt for future reference Fee exemption is available only for eligible categories as per rules

age_limit.details: Minimum Age: 21 years Maximum Age: 40 years Age Calculation Date: 01.01.2026 | SC/ST Candidates: 5 years relaxation OBC Candidates: 3 years relaxation PwD Candidates (Unreserved): 10 years relaxation PwD Candidates (OBC): 13 years relaxation PwD Candidates (SC/ST): 15 years relaxation Ex-Servicemen: Actual service rendered in Armed Forces plus 3 years Women Candidates (Unreserved): 5 years relaxation Women Candidates (OBC): 8 years relaxation Women Candidates (SC/ST): 10 years relaxation MP Domicile Candidates: Age relaxation as per state government rules Upper age limit may be relaxed for candidates as per Government of Madhya Pradesh orders

qualification.details: Candidates must possess a Graduate degree in Agriculture from a recognized University Knowledge of Hindi at High School level (Class 10) is mandatory Candidates should have studied Hindi or Sanskrit at matriculation level or above Degree must be from UGC recognized University or Institution | Bachelor's degree in Agriculture or allied subjects from recognized University Hindi language proficiency at Class 10 level or equivalent Valid domicile certificate of Madhya Pradesh (for reserved category candidates) Must fulfill all the conditions mentioned in the notification | Experience in agricultural extension work Knowledge of farming practices and rural development Working knowledge of agricultural schemes and programs Computer proficiency and MS Office knowledge | No prior experience is mandatory for direct recruitment candidates Fresh agriculture graduates can apply Relevant field experience in agriculture extension will be an added advantage Experience in government agricultural departments will be preferred

vacancy_details.Post Name: Assistant Director, Farmer Welfare & Agriculture Development Department (Field & Extension)

category_wise_vacancy.Post Name: Assistant Director, Farmer Welfare & Agriculture Development Department (Field & Extension)

salary_details.details: Pay Scale: ₹15,600 - ₹39,100 per month Grade Pay: ₹5,400 Pay Band: PB-3 as per pay matrix Dearness Allowance: As applicable as per Government norms House Rent Allowance: As per posting location Transport Allowance: As per Government rules Other Benefits: Medical facilities, LTC, pension benefits and other admissible allowances as per state government rules Annual Increments: As per service rules and regulations

selection_process: The selection will be conducted through a competitive examination process Preliminary Examination: Objective type examination with multiple choice questions Total Questions: 100 questions Total Marks: 200 marks Examination Duration: 2 hours Negative Marking: 1/3 marks will be deducted for each wrong answer Qualifying Nature: The preliminary examination is of qualifying nature Candidates who qualify the preliminary examination will be eligible for the main examination Main Examination: Written examination on subjects related to agriculture and extension Interview/Personality Test: Qualified candidates from main examination will be called for interview Final selection will be based on the combined performance in main examination and interview Merit list will be prepared category-wise based on total marks obtained Selected candidates will be appointed subject to verification of documents and medical fitness
how_to_apply: Step 1: Visit the official website www.mppsc.mp.gov.in or www.mponline.gov.in
Step 2: Click on "Online Application" link for Assistant Director (Farmer Welfare) Examination 2026
Step 3: New candidates must first register by clicking on "New Registration"
Step 4: Provide basic details like name, father's name, date of birth, email ID, and mobile number
Step 5: Create a password and complete the registration process
Step 6: After successful registration, login using Registration ID and password
Step 7: Fill in the application form with all required details carefully
Step 8: Upload scanned photograph (passport size colored), signature, and other required documents
Step 9: Select examination center preference from the available options
Step 10: Verify all entered information thoroughly before final submission
Step 11: Submit the application form after verification
Step 12: After submission, click on "Proceed for Payment"
Step 13: Pay the application fee online through Debit Card/Credit Card/Net Banking/UPI
Step 14: After successful payment, application status will show "Payment Done"
Step 15: Take a printout of the submitted application form and payment receipt
Step 16: Keep the printout and payment receipt safely for future reference
Step 17: Regularly check the official website for updates regarding admit card and examination
Fill all mandatory fields marked with asterisk carefully
Use only English alphabet and numerals while filling the form
Do not use special characters in name and address fields
Photograph should be recent, colored, and passport size (3.5 cm x 4.5 cm)
Signature should be done on white paper with black or blue ink
File size of photograph should not exceed 50 KB
File size of signature should not exceed 20 KB
Supported file formats: JPG/JPEG only
Ensure photograph and signature are clear and not blurred
Do not share login credentials with anyone
Application reference number should be noted for future correspondence
Candidates can make corrections during the specified correction window only
important_instructions: Candidates must be Indian citizens Only online applications will be accepted through official website Candidates must have a valid email ID and mobile number for registration All certificates and documents must be ready in scanned format before applying Photograph and signature should be in prescribed format and size (JPG/JPEG only) Application form once submitted cannot be changed except during correction window Candidates are advised to read the complete notification carefully before applying Any false information provided will lead to cancellation of candidature at any stage The Commission reserves the right to cancel or modify the recruitment process Court jurisdiction will be at Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh only Candidates serving in Government/Semi-Government organizations must produce NOC Physical fitness certificate will be required at the time of appointment Character verification will be conducted for selected candidates Candidates must preserve all original documents till completion of recruitment MPPSC's decision will be final and binding in all matters

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies for MPPSC Assistant Director Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 71 vacancies for the post of Assistant Director in Farmer Welfare and Agriculture Development Department, distributed across various categories including UR, SC, ST, OBC, and EWS with provisions for PwD candidates.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for MPPSC Assistant Director post?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Bachelor's degree in Agriculture or allied subjects from a recognized University with knowledge of Hindi at High School level (Class 10 or above) to be eligible for this position.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for MPPSC Assistant Director Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 21 years and maximum age is 40 years as on 01.01.2026. Age relaxation of 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, and up to 15 years for PwD candidates is applicable as per rules.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for MPPSC Assistant Director Recruitment?
faq.answer: The application fee is ₹3,000/- for UR/OBC/EWS candidates and ₹250/- for SC/ST/PwD candidates of Madhya Pradesh. An additional portal fee of ₹50/- is applicable for all categories and is non-refundable.

faq.question: Q5. What is the last date to apply for MPPSC Assistant Director Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit online application is 24/05/2026 (till 12:00 noon). Candidates are advised to apply well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues and server congestion.
